spec_match.rationale: Keeps the live cold-vs-warm Cache API timing demo and now adds a two-top-level-site model that contrasts shared allowlisted buckets with partitioned self-hosted resources and purged shared entries.

interactive.rationale: Run demo / Clear cache buttons drive a real Cache API + Resource Timing measurement, animating bars and emitting a speedup ratio — past the static-card bar.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The feature index blurb promises 'Cache API and fetch timing to illustrate cache hit versus miss behaviour'; the page does exactly that, even if it under-sells the actual partitioning story.

edge_cases.rationale: Covers allowlisted shared hits, self-hosted non-allowlisted misses, and a simulated shared-cache purge. Lower-level Vary header, opaque response, and range-request edge cases are still not modelled.

references.rationale: The see-also block links to https://chromestatus.com/feature/5202380930678784 directly.

design_system.rationale: All colours come from CSS variables, buttons inherit the global black-on-ivory rule with no overrides, no static-card pattern.

openQuestions.title: Surface transferSize / encodedBodySize / decodedBodySize for memory vs disk cache hits
openQuestions.detail: The code-card mentions 'memory cache (sub-millisecond) vs disk cache (1–5ms)' but the live readout doesn't actually distinguish them. Pull entry.deliveryType once Chrome ships it, or fall back to inferring from durations and surface the cache tier in the timing card.

openQuestions.title: Show what happens after a Cache-Control purge
openQuestions.detail: If a pervasive resource ages out or is removed from the allowlist mid-session, what does the developer see? Add a 'simulate evict' button that clears the entry and re-runs the second fetch as a cold network call.

summary: The page now pairs the live Cache API timing demo with a cross-site partition model that shows an allowlisted resource reusing a shared bucket, a self-hosted lookalike remaining partitioned, and a purged entry falling back to a network miss.
